allusions.surface: let them even be called Philippics
allusions.translator_note: Brutus ratifies Cicero's own joking use of the name 'Philippicae' for his anti-Antonian speeches; the allusion to Demosthenes' orations against Philip II of Macedon is deliberate and flattering, casting Cicero as the Athenian champion of liberty and Antony as a new Philip.
